entityName: Love, Chunibyo & Other Delusions: Heart Throb

metadata.entityName: Love, Chunibyo & Other Delusions: Heart Throb

ingredients.name: Karahashi Park
ingredients.address: Karahashi Park, 1 Chome-27 Seta, Otsu, Shiga 520-2134, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This park along the Seta River doubles as the neighborhood backdrop near Rikka and Yuuta's apartment complex, appearing in the opening animation with its distinctive bridge silhouette. Fans visit to recreate the calm riverside shots that frame Rikka's daily commute.

ingredients.name: Hirakata Park
ingredients.address: Hirakata Park, 1-1 Hirakatakoencho, Hirakata, Osaka 573-0054, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The gang visits this amusement park in episode 10 for what becomes an awkward group outing as Yuuta navigates his feelings amid Satone's growing presence. The rollercoaster and festival-game scenes here mark a turning point in the season's romantic tension.

ingredients.name: North Inner Enclosure
ingredients.localName: 吉野ヶ里遺跡 北内郭
ingredients.entityContext: This inner ceremonial compound of the Yoshinogari site appears in episode 6 as the dramatic stage for Satone's elaborate fantasy enactment, with its elevated platform and thatched roof giving the scene an otherworldly atmosphere. It is the most-filmed specific structure from the Yoshinogari episode.

ingredients.name: Onsen Hotel Nakahara Bessou
ingredients.address: Onsen Hotel Nakahara Bessou, 2F 15-19 Terukunicho, Kagoshima, 892-0841, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Kagoshima inn serves as the model for the hotel where the class stays during the school trip in episode 6, with its distinctive entrance and corridor layout referenced in interior scenes. Fans compare the lobby details as one of the trip arc's confirmed location sources.

ingredients.name: Japan Post Bank Kagoshima Branch
ingredients.localName: ゆうちょ銀行 鹿児島店
ingredients.address: Japan Post Bank Kagoshima Branch, 3-3 Yamashitacho, Kagoshima, 892-8799, Japan

ingredients.name: Tenmonkan Mujaki
ingredients.localName: 天文館むじゃき 本店
ingredients.address: Tenmonkan Mujaki, 5-8 Sennichicho, Kagoshima, 892-0843, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This landmark Kagoshima dessert shop, famous for its shirokuma shaved ice, appears in episode 7 when the class tries Kagoshima's iconic sweet. Rikka's over-the-top reaction to the colorful dessert became a fan-favorite screenshot from the trip arc.

ingredients.name: Statue of Saigo Takamori
ingredients.address: Statue of Saigō, Ueno Park, 7 Chome-7-7 Uenokoen, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0007, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 7 Satone drags the group to see this famous Ueno statue during a Tokyo detour, using it as a backdrop for one of her chuunibyou proclamations. The scene highlights her performative side against a recognisable national landmark.

ingredients.name: Shiroyama Park Observation Deck
ingredients.address: Shiroyama Park Observation Deck, 22-13 Shiroyamacho, Kagoshima, 892-0853, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: During the class trip to Kagoshima in episode 7, this hilltop observation deck delivers the panoramic view of the city and Sakurajima volcano that Rikka gazes at with characteristic theatricality. The sweeping vista shot is one of the most reproduced comparison photos in Chunibyo pilgrimage reports.

ingredients.name: Fujimi City Center
ingredients.address: Fujimi City Hall, 1800-1 Tsuruma, Fujimi, Saitama 354-8511, Japan

ingredients.name: Toray Engineering West Japan
ingredients.localName: 東レエンジニアリング 西日本
ingredients.address: Toray Engineering West Japan, 1 Chome-1-1 Sonoyama, Otsu, Shiga 520-0842, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This corporate building in Otsu's Sonoyama district appears in episode 11 as background scenery during a neighborhood walk sequence, its modern facade visible in shots that establish the industrial-residential character of the Seta area. Fans note it as one of several Otsu background buildings confirmed in the episode.

ingredients.name: Toray Arena
ingredients.address: Toray Arena, 2 Chome-8-1 Sonoyama, Otsu, Shiga 520-0842, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This sports arena in Otsu near the Seta area appears in episode 11 as a background landmark during outdoor scenes in the characters' neighborhood, its distinctive roofline visible in wider establishing shots. It is one of several Otsu landmarks anchoring the show's real-world geography.

ingredients.name: Kyoto Aquarium
ingredients.address: Kyoto Aquarium, 梅小路公園, 内, 35-1 Kankijicho, Shimogyo Ward, Kyoto, 600-8835, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yuuta takes Rikka on an impromptu outing to this aquarium in episode 2, where she watches the jellyfish display with wide-eyed wonder, momentarily dropping her chuunibyou persona in quiet amazement. The jellyfish tank scene is one of the series' most tender early moments between the two.

ingredients.name: Fujimi Civic Heated Swimming Pool
ingredients.localName: 富士見市民温水プール

ingredients.name: Fujimi Civic Heated Swimming Pool
ingredients.localName: 富士見市民温水プール

ingredients.name: Fujimi Civic Heated Swimming Pool
ingredients.localName: 富士見市民温水プール

ingredients.name: Fujimi Civic Heated Swimming Pool
ingredients.localName: 富士見市民温水プール

ingredients.name: Akanoi-1 Parking lot
ingredients.entityContext: This lakeside parking area near the Akanoi wetland along Lake Biwa appears in episode 2 as the staging point for Yuuta and Rikka's lakeside walk, with the open water view and reedy shoreline matching shots from the episode. It connects to the Karasuma Peninsula sequence.

ingredients.name: Karasuma Takeout Café Reed
ingredients.address: Karasuma Takeout Café Reed, 烏丸半島内 1091 Oroshimocho, Kusatsu, Shiga 525-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Koganryokuchitsuda - 1 Kita Parking Lot
ingredients.entityContext: This car park near the Tsuda Inlet of Lake Biwa appears in episode 2 during the lakeside outing, providing access to the open water and reed-bed scenery filmed for Rikka and Yuuta's walk. It is typically visited as part of the same Lake Biwa pilgrimage route as Karasuma Reed.

ingredients.name: Fresh and cheap market! Kohata
ingredients.localName: 新鮮激安市場！ 木幡店

ingredients.name: Houseboat Shiki
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional houseboat moored near the Seta River appears in episode 1 as riverside scenery framing an early scene between Rikka and Yuuta near their neighborhood. The distinctive roof silhouette of the boat is matched in fan comparison posts.

ingredients.name: Beniya Mukayu Ryokan
ingredients.address: Beniya Mukayu Ryokan, 55-1-3 Yamashiroonsen, Kaga, Ishikawa 922-0242, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This high-end ryokan in Yamanaka Onsen, Ishikawa is referenced as the visual model for the inn setting in episode 1 where a brief fantasy sequence is staged, its elegant garden corridor appearing in background details. Dedicated pilgrims include it in Season 2 location guides.

ingredients.name: Houseboat Shiki
ingredients.entityContext: This traditional houseboat moored near the Seta River appears in episode 1 as riverside scenery framing an early scene between Rikka and Yuuta near their neighborhood. The distinctive roof silhouette of the boat is matched in fan comparison posts.

ingredients.name: UR Momoyama Minami
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒612-8006 Kyoto, Fushimi Ward, Momoyamacho Oshima, UR 桃山南団地 ２１
ingredients.entityContext: This apartment complex in Fushimi, Kyoto serves as the reference model for the residential building where Rikka's family connection in Kyoto is shown in episode 2, its concrete block facade and walkways appearing in establishing shots. Local fans include it in Kyoto-route pilgrimage guides.

ingredients.name: McDonald's Ishiyama Station
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド 石山駅前店
ingredients.address: McDonald's Ishiyama Station, 9-1 Awazucho, Otsu, Shiga 520-0832, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yuuta and Rikka stop at this fast food restaurant near Ishiyama Station in episode 4, providing a mundane contrast to Rikka's elaborate chuunibyou worldview as she insists on ordering with dramatic flair. The exterior and interior angles match closely enough that fans document it as a confirmed shooting reference.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Tamanoura
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 福山野上町店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart; Fukuyama Nogamicho, 2 Chome-7-4 Nogamicho, Fukuyama, Hiroshima 720-0815, Japan

ingredients.name: Kaigake Elementary School
ingredients.localName: しゃくなげ学校(旧鎌掛小学校)
ingredients.address: Kaigake Elementary School, 2362 Kaigake, Hino, Gamo District, Shiga 529-1631, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This picturesque old wooden school building in rural Shiga appears in episode 1 as part of the countryside scenery establishing Rikka's family backstory, its weathered facade evoking the nostalgic world apart from the city. It is one of the more remote pilgrimage stops attracting dedicated fans.

ingredients.name: Anoo Station
ingredients.address: Anoo Station, 2-chōme-8 Anou, Otsu, Shiga 520-0114, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This quiet Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto line station appears in episode 3 during a scene where characters travel along the lake shore, its small unmanned platform capturing the sleepy suburban atmosphere of the show's Otsu setting. Fans visit for the characteristic lakeside rail scenery.

ingredients.name: Keihan-Ishiyama Station
ingredients.address: Keihan-Ishiyama Station, 2 Awazuchō, Otsu, Shiga 520-0832, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small Keihan line station near the characters' apartment complex appears repeatedly as the launchpad for Rikka and Yuuta's outings, including the episode 1 arrival scene. It is the most consistently filmed transit spot in the Otsu pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Kusatsushi Public Library
ingredients.address: Kusatsushi Public Library, 1547 Kusatsucho, Kusatsu, Shiga 525-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The exterior of this library stands in for the school library area in episode 10, where characters have a quiet confrontation about their feelings as the season's romantic arc builds toward its resolution. The low-slung building and entrance are matched in fan comparison shots.

ingredients.name: Hiyama Shrine
ingredients.address: Oyama Shrine Peak Sanctuary, Ashikuraji, Tateyama, Nakaniikawa District, Toyama 930-1406, Japan

ingredients.name: Setahigashi City Center
ingredients.address: Setahigashi City Center, 大津市瀬田東支所 3 Chome-16-1 Ichiriyama, Otsu, Shiga 520-2153, Japan

ingredients.name: Waterfront Park
ingredients.localName: ウォーターフロントパーク

ingredients.name: Waterfront Park
ingredients.localName: ウォーターフロントパーク

ingredients.name: Waterfront Park
ingredients.localName: ウォーターフロントパーク

ingredients.name: Chuo Park
ingredients.address: Shinjuku Chūō Park, 2 Chome-11 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan

ingredients.name: Kagoshima Medical Center
ingredients.localName: 国立病院機構 鹿児島医療センター
ingredients.address: Kagoshima Medical Center, 8-1 Shiroyamacho, Kagoshima, 892-0853, Japan

ingredients.name: Cave of Takamori Saigo (Satsuma Rebellion 1877)
ingredients.address: Cave of Takamori Saigo (Satsuma Rebellion 1877), 19 Shiroyamacho, Kagoshima, 892-0853, Japan

ingredients.name: Satsuma Retainers Monument
ingredients.address: Satsuma Retainers Monument, 11 Shiroyamacho, Kagoshima, 892-0853, Japan

ingredients.name: Shiroyama Park
ingredients.address: Shiroyama Park, 22-33 Shiroyamacho, Kagoshima, 892-0853, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The wooded slopes of this park surrounding the Kagoshima castle hill appear in episode 7 as the group explores the area, with dappled forest paths providing atmosphere during a quieter moment between Yuuta and Rikka away from the class. The park connects several of the Kagoshima filming locations.

ingredients.name: Minamikyushu Chozai Pharmacy
ingredients.localName: しんまち薬局 |南九州 薬局 漢方薬局 薬草 処方箋 土曜日 日曜日|
ingredients.address: しんまち薬局 |南九州 薬局 漢方薬局 薬草 処方箋 土曜日 日曜日|, 3968-1 Kawanabecho Tabeta, Minamikyushu, Kagoshima 897-0221, Japan

ingredients.name: Yoshinogari Historical Park
ingredients.address: Yoshinogari Historical Park, 1843 Tade, Yoshinogari, Kanzaki District, Saga 842-0035, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The class field trip in episode 6 stops at this reconstructed Yayoi-period village, where Satone (Shichimiya Sopherta) seizes on the ancient watchtowers and ritual enclosures as perfect props for her chuunibyou role-play, much to the class's bewilderment. The distinctive thatched structures are unmistakable in the comparison shots.

ingredients.name: South inner enclosure, North watchtower
ingredients.address: South inner enclosure, North watchtower, Tade, Kanzaki, Kanzaki District, Saga 842-0035, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The north watchtower of the south enclosure at Yoshinogari is featured in episode 6 as Satone climbs toward it during her chuunibyou performance, the wooden observation tower framing her silhouette against the sky. Pilgrims photograph this exact angle to recreate the scene.

ingredients.name: Lake Biwa senmatsu
ingredients.address: Lake Biwa First Canal, 7 Miideracho, Otsu, Shiga 520-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic first canal flowing out of Lake Biwa appears in the opening animation sequence, its tree-lined stone channel providing the scenic waterway image that many fans associate with the show's Shiga identity. It is one of the clearest OP location matches for the series.
